about.title: About Juhuri Heritage
about.welcomeText: An innovative digital project to preserve, document, and share the Juhuri language (Judeo-Tat) — the language of the Mountain Jews of the Caucasus. Combining academic research with modern AI technology.
about.whatIsText1: Juhuri (also known as Judeo-Tat) is an ancient Jewish language from the Iranian language family, spoken for centuries by the Mountain Jews (Caucasus Jews) in what is now Azerbaijan and Dagestan. It is closely related to Persian but evolved with Hebrew and Aramaic influences.
about.whatIsText2: Today Juhuri is considered an endangered language, with a declining number of speakers worldwide. Large Mountain Jewish communities live in Israel, New York, Moscow, and Baku.
about.dictionaryText: The Juhuri Heritage Dictionary is the largest digital Juhuri dictionary, containing over 46,000 entries with translations into Hebrew, English, and Russian, Latin transliteration, pronunciation guides, and vowel markings. Built from academic research and community contributions.
about.missionText: We believe that a language is the soul of a community. Preserving Juhuri is essential for passing on the heritage of the Mountain Jews from generation to generation. Our goal is to provide a comprehensive and accessible digital platform for learning, documenting, and celebrating Juhuri culture.
about.teamText: Behind Juhuri Heritage is a dedicated team of linguists, developers, and community members. We are always looking for new volunteers.
about.timeline1Text: Jews settle in the Caucasus region, mainly in Derbent and Azerbaijan. The Juhuri language develops as a distinct language from Persian.
about.timeline2Text: Mountain Jewish communities thrive across the Caucasus. The Juhuri language develops rich literature, poetry, and oral tradition.
about.timeline3Text: Under Soviet rule, Juhuri transitions to Latinization and then Cyrillicization. The community preserves its identity despite pressures.
about.timeline4Text: Large waves of emigration to Israel, New York, and Moscow. The Juhuri language begins to be displaced by Hebrew, Russian, and English.
about.timeline5Text: Juhuri Heritage launches — the largest digital dictionary with over 46,000 entries, preserving the language for future generations.
about.howStep1Text: Type a word in any language — Hebrew, Juhuri, Russian, or Latin transliteration.
about.howStep2Text: Get translations, pronunciation, usage examples, and dialect information.
about.howStep3Text: Add new words, correct translations, and help preserve the language.
about.howStep4Text: Use the AI tutor to learn Juhuri at your own pace.
about.mapIsraelText: The largest community — Haifa, Or Akiva, Be'er Sheva
about.mapUSAText: New York (Brooklyn, Queens) — the largest community outside Israel
about.mapRussiaText: Moscow, Derbent (Dagestan) — historic place of origin
about.mapAzerbaijanText: Baku, Quba ("Red Town") — historic center of Mountain Jews
